events.title: Unification of Egypt
events.description: Narmer unifies Upper and Lower Egypt into the world's first nation-state.

events.title: The Old Kingdom
events.description: The Pyramid Age marked by divine kingship and monumental construction.

events.title: The Middle Kingdom
events.description: Egypt's Classical Age characterized by literature and rulers acting as 'shepherds'.

events.title: Abraham in Egypt
events.description: Abraham seeks refuge in Egypt during a famine; Pharaoh's house is plagued.

events.title: Hyksos Rule (Second Intermediate Period)
events.description: Foreign rulers introduce the chariot; Joseph likely rises to power during this era.

events.title: The New Kingdom
events.description: The Empire Age, marked by great wealth, warrior-kings, and monumental temples.

events.title: The Exodus
events.description: God liberates Israel from Egyptian bondage.

events.title: Alexander the Great Conquers Egypt
events.description: Egypt is liberated from Persian rule and the Hellenistic period begins.

events.title: Death of Cleopatra VII
events.description: End of the Ptolemaic dynasty; Egypt becomes a Roman province.
